title: Cannot perform a `podman system add` between two macOS hosts
body:
### Issue Description

I'm trying to connect a podman running on a Mac M1 to a podman on a Mac Intel but I receive a strange bash-related error every time.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. ensure SSH keys are correctly set up on both hosts and SSH connections are working
2. perform a `podman system connection add <host> ssh://<user>@<host>.local`

### Describe the results you received

```
Error: bash: ligne 1: podman : commande introuvable
: Process exited with status 127
```

### Describe the results you expected

That the system connection to the remote Mac is added.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Both Macs are using MacPorts' `podman` port, meaning the `podman` command is located in `/opt/local/bin/podman`.

### Additional information

On the remote Intel Mac, I tried the following workaround with no success:

1. adding the `/opt/local/bin` to PATH in `.bashrc`, `.bash_profile`, `.profile`
2. adding a symlink to `/opt/local/bin/podman*` in `/usr/local/bin`

comments.body: I don't know what the exact error is but I am somewhat sure you cannot connect to another mac (remote client), the system connection must connect to the actual podman service running on linux.

OK, I found a solution...

Not sure it's necessary but I created a new password-less SSH key and copied it to the remote mac.

Then:
```
$ ssh <username>@<remote-mac> "/opt/local/bin/podman machine inspect --format '{{.ConnectionInfo.PodmanSocket}}'"
{/var/folders/ny/tnjvr5kx4nd6c9c8kkwnb2cw0000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default-api.sock <nil>}

$ podman system connection add <remote-mac> ssh://<username>@<remote-mac>/var/folders/ny/tnjvr5kx4nd6c9c8kkwnb2cw0000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default-api.sock --identity ~/.ssh/id_podman

$ podman --connection <remote-mac> ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES

$ podman --connection <remote-mac> version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.5.2
API Version:  5.5.2
Go Version:   go1.24.5
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.3.1
API Version:  5.3.1
Go Version:   go1.22.7
Built:        Thu Nov 21 01:00:00 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Tricky... but it works.

Indeed, a password-less SSH key is not necessary:

```
$ podman system connection add <remote-mac> ssh://<username>@<remote-mac>/var/folders/ny/tnjvr5kx4nd6c9c8kkwnb2cw0000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default-api.sock

$ podman --connection <remote-mac> version
Key Passphrase:
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.5.2
API Version:  5.5.2
Go Version:   go1.24.5
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.3.1
API Version:  5.3.1
Go Version:   go1.22.7
Built:        Thu Nov 21 01:00:00 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
